definitions.definition: Chemical kinetics is the study of the rates of chemical reactions and the factors that influence these rates.
The rate of reaction is the change in concentration of reactants or products per unit time.
Rate of reaction is the rate at which reactants are converted to products.
Molecularity of a reaction is the number of molecules of reactants that participate in the reaction.
The unit of rate of reaction is mole per liter per second (mol L-1 s-1) or concentration per unit time.
The order of a reaction is the sum of the exponents of the concentration terms in the rate equation.

definitions.term: Order of Reaction with Respect to a Reactant
definitions.definition: The order of reaction with respect to a reactant is the exponent of the concentration term of that reactant in the rate equation.
Half-life period of a reaction is the time required for the concentration of reactants to fall to half of their initial value.
Rate constant (K) is a measure of the rate of a reaction and depends on the temperature and the nature of the reactants.
The Arrhenius equation relates the rate constant of a reaction to the activation energy and temperature.
Activation energy (Ea) is the minimum energy required for the reactant molecules to overcome the energy barrier and form products.
Collision theory states that a chemical reaction occurs when the reactant molecules collide with each other with sufficient energy and proper orientation.
Factors affecting rate of reaction include concentration of reactants, surface area, temperature, and catalyst.
A catalyst is a substance that speeds up a chemical reaction without being consumed or altered in the process.
An inhibitor is a substance that slows down or prevents a chemical reaction from occurring.
Equilibrium constant (Kc) is a measure of the ratio of the concentrations of products to reactants at equilibrium.
